message:
perf(mvcc/vdbe): predicate-only synthetic-conflict probe avoids ConcurrentPageState clone

Adds a `concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only` predicate to the
mvcc public surface and rewires the two VDBE hot paths that were
calling `concurrent_page_state(...).is_synthetic_conflict_only()`
purely for the boolean answer.

Why it matters: `concurrent_page_state` is a constructor — it walks
the handle's page entry, clones any `staged_data`, and packages the
result into a `ConcurrentPageState` enum. For a page with megabytes
of staged write data, that clone is real work. The two call sites in
the VDBE cleanup path don't care about the staged data; they only
need to know "is page 1 still a synthetic conflict-only marker?"

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/begin_concurrent.rs (+15 lines, +8 test lines):

  pub fn concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only(
      handle: &ConcurrentHandle,
      page: PageNumber,
  ) -> bool {
      handle.page_state(page).is_some_and(|state| {
          state.is_conflict_only
              && state.staged_data.is_none()
              && !state.is_freed
      })
  }

The predicate is `#[must_use]` and has a doc comment that calls out
the optimization rationale ("hot cleanup paths use it when they only
need the boolean answer, avoiding a full `ConcurrentPageState`
construction and possible staged-page clone").

The existing `concurrent_clear_page_state` test is extended with two
new asserts that guarantee the predicate is consistent with the
existing `tracks_write_conflict_page` semantics:
  - After registering a conflict-only page: predicate returns true.
  - After `concurrent_clear_page_state`: predicate returns false.
The second assertion has the same shape as the existing
`!handle.held_locks().contains(...)` assertion, so when the cleanup
path regresses, both checks fail in lock-step and the failure
attribution stays clean.

crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lib.rs:

  - Exposes `concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only` from the
    public re-export block (alphabetical order, between
    `concurrent_page_is_freed` and `concurrent_page_read_state`).
  - Imports list above the test mod gets the same insertion so the
    crate's internal modules can see the new predicate.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs (-2/+2 substantive):

  Two call sites in `SharedTxnPageIo` (the txn-shared page IO that
  backs the VDBE engine's stale-page-1 cleanup):

    - Pre-check (line 1810): used to do
        `concurrent_page_state(&handle, PageNumber::ONE)
            .is_synthetic_conflict_only()`
      Now does
        `concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only(&handle,
            PageNumber::ONE)`.
      The pre-check exits early when page 1 isn't synthetic, so we
      skip the cleanup entirely; the cheaper predicate fits exactly.
    - Re-check inside the lock (line 1822): same swap. This second
      check exists to guard against another writer racing in between
      the read-only pre-check and the upgrade-to-write lock; using
      the predicate here also avoids an extra clone while we're
      already holding the write lock.

The `use` block of `engine.rs` gets the same import addition.

Public API: additive only — no existing function changed shape, no
behavior change at any call site, no SSI / FCW invariants touched.
The new predicate is a pure read of the page state and can be
compiled out at any point if the underlying field model ever
changes.

files.patch:
@@ -1305,6 +1305,21 @@ pub fn concurrent_page_state(handle: &ConcurrentHandle, page: PageNumber) -> Con
     }
 }
 
+/// Return whether a page is tracked only as a synthetic conflict surface.
+///
+/// This is the predicate-only companion to [`concurrent_page_state`]. Hot
+/// cleanup paths use it when they only need the boolean answer, avoiding a
+/// full `ConcurrentPageState` construction and possible staged-page clone.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only(
+    handle: &ConcurrentHandle,
+    page: PageNumber,
+) -> bool {
+    handle.page_state(page).is_some_and(|state| {
+        state.is_conflict_only && state.staged_data.is_none() && !state.is_freed
+    })
+}
+
 /// Restore a page's local concurrent tracking state after a failed pager write.
 pub fn concurrent_restore_page_state(
     handle: &mut ConcurrentHandle,
@@ -3522,8 +3537,9 @@ mod tests {
         ConcurrentRegistry, FcwResult, HandleView, MAX_CONCURRENT_WRITERS, concurrent_abort,
         concurrent_clear_page_state, concurrent_commit, concurrent_commit_with_ssi,
         concurrent_free_page, concurrent_is_metadata_exempt, concurrent_mark_metadata_exempt,
-        concurrent_page_is_freed, concurrent_page_state, concurrent_read_page,
-        concurrent_restore_page_state, concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint, concurrent_savepoint,
+        concurrent_page_is_freed, concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only,
+        concurrent_page_state, concurrent_read_page, concurrent_restore_page_state,
+        concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint, concurrent_savepoint,
         concurrent_track_write_conflict_page, concurrent_write_metadata_page,
         concurrent_write_page, finalize_prepared_concurrent_commit_with_ssi,
         prepare_concurrent_commit_with_ssi, summarize_witness_keys, validate_first_committer_wins,
@@ -3924,13 +3940,21 @@ mod tests {
 
         assert!(handle.tracks_write_conflict_page(PageNumber::ONE));
         assert!(handle.held_locks().contains(&PageNumber::ONE));
+        assert!(
+            concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only(&handle, PageNumber::ONE),
+            "a conflict-only page with no staged data or free marker is synthetic"
+        );
 
         concurrent_clear_page_state(&mut handle, &lock_table, s1, PageNumber::ONE).unwrap();
 
         assert!(
             !handle.tracks_write_conflict_page(PageNumber::ONE),
             "clearing the page state must remove the synthetic conflict surface"
         );
+        assert!(
+            !concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only(&handle, PageNumber::ONE),
+            "clearing the page state must clear the synthetic predicate"
+        );
         assert!(
             !handle.held_locks().contains(&PageNumber::ONE),
             "clearing the page state must release the page lock"

@@ -189,11 +189,12 @@ pub use begin_concurrent::{
     MAX_CONCURRENT_WRITERS, PreparedConcurrentCommit, SharedConcurrentHandle, SsiResult,
     concurrent_abort, concurrent_clear_page_state, concurrent_commit, concurrent_commit_with_ssi,
     concurrent_free_page, concurrent_has_page_state, concurrent_is_metadata_exempt,
-    concurrent_mark_metadata_exempt, concurrent_page_is_freed, concurrent_page_read_state,
-    concurrent_page_state, concurrent_prepare_write_page, concurrent_read_page,
-    concurrent_record_metadata_read, concurrent_restore_page_state,
-    concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint, concurrent_savepoint, concurrent_stage_prepared_write_page,
-    concurrent_track_write_conflict_page, concurrent_write_metadata_page, concurrent_write_page,
+    concurrent_mark_metadata_exempt, concurrent_page_is_freed,
+    concurrent_page_is_synthetic_conflict_only, concurrent_page_read_state, concurrent_page_state,
+    concurrent_prepare_write_page, concurrent_read_page, concurrent_record_metadata_read,
+    concurrent_restore_page_state, concurrent_rollback_to_savepoint, concurrent_savepoint,
+    concurrent_stage_prepared_write_page, concurrent_track_write_conflict_page,
+    concurrent_write_metadata_page, concurrent_write_page,
     finalize_prepared_concurrent_commit_with_ssi, is_concurrent_mode,
     prepare_concurrent_commit_fcw_only, prepare_concurrent_commit_with_ssi,
     validate_first_committer_wins,
